text.title: Faion CLI Agent Adapter Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Faion CLI Agent Adapter Pattern application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Package MUST ship adapters for at least 3 targets: Anthropic tool, LangChain Tool, OpenAI function. Single-target adapters are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cross-SDK compatibility is the point; single-target shows up as 'works on the author's stack only' in the wild.

rule.statement: All adapters MUST share an error-handling layer that maps faion-CLI exit codes / HTTP codes to typed exceptions surfaced to the agent. Per-adapter ad-hoc handling is rejected.
rule.rationale: Per-adapter error logic drifts; the agent learns inconsistent behaviour; the shared layer makes errors predictable.

rule.statement: Adapters MUST cache `get-content` responses by (slug, version) for the agent session. Uncached repeated lookups are rejected.
rule.rationale: Repeated lookups burn latency and quota; agents call the same slug many times per task; the cache is a 5x perf win on average.

rule.statement: Each adapter MUST accept and forward an explicit version to faion-CLI. Calls without a version are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without version pinning, the cited content can shift under the agent mid-task; pinning preserves reproducibility.

rule.statement: Adapter MUST emit a `[faion:<slug>@<version>]` citation in the tool response so the agent can include it in its output by default. Citation-less responses are rejected.
rule.rationale: Citation-contract methodology is a hard dependency; wiring it at adapter level makes the attribution automatic rather than opt-in.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
